entry.title: Re: Error message :Grouping has external number assignmen...
entry.content:
Please check the setting in the following configuration setting:

  1. Open the Manage Your Solution app.
  2. Open the configuration step Define Groupings and Assign Number Ranges (SSCUI 102241)
  3. Change the Number Range to an internal number range and the business partner will be created with the next available number

OR 

Enter the next available external number.

For further information see Business Partner Number Grouping

entry.title: Where can I learn the business processes from the contract accounting area?
entry.content:
I  recommend to access our Implementing SAP Financial Contract Accounting learning journey.

Overview
This Learning Journey provides an overview of the features of SAP Financial Contract Accounting (FI-CA) in the context of the SAP Billing and Revenue Innovation Management solution (BRIM).

Learning objectives
After completing this intermediate level learning journey, Implementation Consultants will understand the most important business processes from the contract accounting area that are represented in the system.
Also they will understand the technical data context of contract accounts subledger and its integration into FI general ledger accounting.

Prerequisites
  • Discovering the capabilities of SAP Solutions for Quote-to-Cash Management private cloud (BRIM) for Business User

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area. 

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 
I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Re: Can user has user defined header/item condition in Sa...
entry.content:
Hello @Ravi_Mehta 

Based on the screenshot you shared earlier for the PN00 condition type, it appears that the Condition Class was set to B (Price). As a result, the system deactivated the PR00 and K007 condition types. This happens because, within a single pricing procedure, it is not possible to have two condition types with Condition Class B active simultaneously.

Based on the screenshot, I’ve made some changes. Please check and share your feedback

 

Best Regards

Vivek Savant

entry.title: Re: Maximum Limit on Rebate | Settlement Management | Con...
entry.content:
Hi @priya_sshekhar 

Below are solutions for the scenarios you described:

1. Restricting the settlement amount for a rebate with a maximum value:
To achieve this, you can:

  • Either use Graduated scale, - Not supported in standard in SAP CCM
  • Or, Use a fixed amount condition to represent the cap value (e.g., $1000), ensuring the rebate doesn’t exceed this amount. Combine this with condition exclusion to prevent overlapping or conflicting conditions.

The second solution is achievable using standard SAP CCM configuration.

2. Applying rebates only on the quantity above the scale value:
This scenario requires the use of graduated scales. However, SAP CCM doesn’t support graduated scales in standard functionality.

To implement this:

  • A custom pricing routine would need to be developed. This routine would enable the system to calculate rebates based only on the quantities/amount exceeding the defined scale value 

Supporting KBA for confirmation that Graduated scales is not supported: 
2598234 - Graduate Scales are not supported for settlement documents
2678613 - Using Graduated-to interval scales in Condition Contracts 

I hope this clarifies the possibilities and required configurations. 

Best regards,
Gauthier

entry.title: Re: F110: Document was paid although it was blocked
entry.content: I have checked that no one have could explain why this occurs. So I try to clear the issue: if you use the reference invoice, the system uses the data from the referenced invoice in order to execute the payment. Also, if you remove the payment block from the referenced invoice, you don't need to remove from the second document, including if you try to change it, the system will displays a message to inform you that the payment data from the reference invoice will be copied.

entry.title: Re: Account determination for KORS
entry.content:
Hi @lisbeth_z 

Thank you for your question! Let me clarify the behavior of KORS and provide guidance on your scenario.

KORS is not a pricing condition type that you configure in the pricing procedure. Instead, it is an Account Determination Type used for General Ledger (G/L) account determination in SAP. Similar to KOFI, which is generally used for postings to Finance in SAP SD, SAP CCM has its own Account Determination Types such as KORS, KORP, KORR, and KORC, each serving different purposes in rebate and settlement processes.

Configuring Different Accounts for KORS

While you can assign different account keys for various condition types in the pricing procedure, I recommend carefully reviewing your configuration. This is because setting up different accounts for each condition type would require duplicating several critical condition types, such as RES2, REA2, RED2, REA5.

These condition types play a vital role in the calculation and reversal of rebates and accruals. Any misconfiguration can lead to incorrect postings, so ensure that you align your account keys and G/L assignments with the relevant reversal and settlement logic.

Recommendation

It is best practice to use Condition Tables 015 and 016 for maintaining account keys and G/L determination for SAP CCM. These tables simplify account assignment and ensure consistency across different Account Determination Types.

Please also review the configuration guide provided by SAP: 
2535889 - CCM - Description of delivered configuration

Best regards,
Gauthier

entry.title: Consuming Data from Azure Data Lake Storage to Datasphere
entry.content:
Prerequisites: 

  • Access: Ensure access to both SAP Datasphere and ADLS. 
  • Azure Credentials: Have the ADLS storage account name, key, or other authentication methods ready. 

Steps: 

  • Login to SAP Datasphere: 
  • Access your SAP Datasphere instance via the web browser. 
  • Navigate to Connections: 
  • Go to the Connections section from the main menu. 

  • Add a New Connection: 
  • Click on the Connections tab and select Create Connection. 

  • From the list of available connections, choose Azure Data Lake Storage. 

  • Configure ADLS Connection: 
  • Name: Provide a name for your connection. 
  • Azure Storage Account Name: Enter the name of your ADLS storage account. 
  • Authentication Type: Select the type of authentication (e.g., Shared Key, SAS Token, Managed Identity). 
  • Account Key or Token: Enter the relevant key or token. 

  • Test the Connection: 
  • Test the connection to ensure everything is configured correctly. 

 Import Data: 

  • Once the connection is established, you can create data flows or import data from ADLS into SAP Datasphere. 
  • Map the data fields and define how the data should be stored or transformed within Datasphere.

entry.title: Consuming Data from Databricks to SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
Prerequisites 

Either ONE of the following setup options needs to be completed: 

Performed setup using the SAP Analytics Cloud Agent Simple Deployment Kit.  

 

For more information, see SAP Analytics Cloud Agent Simple Deployment Kit. 

Note 

JDBC drivers must be installed using the instructions in the Post-Setup Guide included in the kit. 

 

OR 

 

Perform a manual setup of the following required components: 

  • The Cloud Connector is installed. For more information, see Installing the Cloud Connector. 
    • The SAP Analytics Cloud agent is installed. For more information, see Installing SAP Analytics Cloud Agent. 
      • JDBC drivers must be installed. 
        • The Cloud Connector is configured. For more information, see Configuring the Cloud Connector. 
          • The SAP Analytics Cloud agent is configured in SAP Analytics Cloud. For more information, see Configuring SAP Analytics Cloud Agent. 
            • You have installed a JDBC driver. If you haven't already installed a JDBC driver, follow steps : Import Data Connection to an SQL Database | SAP Help Portal 
              • You must restart the SAP Analytics Cloud agent, using either the Java option -DSAP_CLOUD_AGENT_PROPERTIES_PATH (if the agent is installed on Linux), or an environment variable SAP_CLOUD_AGENT_PROPERTIES_PATH (if the agent is installed on Windows), to specify the complete path up to and including the properties file. Example path: C:\<full path to file>\DriverConfig.properties. Refer: Import Data Connection to an SQL Database | SAP Help Portal 

                Procedure 

                • In the Modeler app, while creating a new model or importing data into an existing SQL Database model, choose Get from a Data Source or Data source. 
                  • Select SQL Databases. 

                     

                     

                    The Create Model from SQL Databases dialog will appear.

entry.title: How to write "IF THEN ELSE" statement with BEX query Key Figure technical ID object in SAC
entry.content:
Hello,

I am trying to create a calculation in SAP analytics cloud on story level which is 

• IF Net Invoice Value is negative then “Credit memo”

My source is BEx query and I have a key figure in the BEx query i.e "Net Invoice Value" which I want to use in the calculated measure.

The technical id of Net Invoice Value in the BEX query is [00O2TKK8G8XRQO3VD5VBZC115]

I tried the following syntax while creating the calculated measure on a story level i.e

IF([00O2TKK8G8XRQO3VD5VBZC115] < 0, "Credit Memo", "")

but I am getting an error message below while writing this logic.

"Formula could not be computed: Object is unknown: 00O2TKK8G8XRQO3VD5VBZC115. Please complete or rewrite the formula".

I will appreciate if you let me know what is wrong with the formula and how to use BEx object in the SAC calculation which has technical ID associated with it.

Thank You very much in advance.

Regards

entry.title: Premium Hub CoE – DSC Knowledge Bits Series – Field Catalogs for Transportation Cockpit in SAP TM
entry.content:
Define Field Catalogs for Transportation Cockpit app is available in Feature Package FPS01 for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2023.

This app provides the functionality to define the available fields of the Transportation Cockpit app including their sequences and names to end users or administrators.

This SAP blog is a step-by-step guide ‘How to work with Define Field Catalogs for Transportation Cockpit app’ and it is organized as follows:

  • Scenario #1. How to add/hide a standard field of Freight Order in Transportation Cockpit app.
  • Scenario #2. How to add a custom field of Freight Order in Transportation Cockpit app.
  • Scenario #3. How to add a native custom field in Transportation Cockpit app.

Scenario #1. How to add/hide a standard field of Freight Order in Transportation Cockpit app.

In this section we will add and hide existing standard fields of Freight Order in Transportation Cockpit app. First, let’s have a look at Transportation Cockpit app. We are going to hide a field ‘Driver’ (e.g. the field is not used by end users).

 

Also, if we have a look at a Freight order structure in BOBF transaction, we are going to make a field ‘PURCH_ORG_ID’ visible.

 

And a field ‘FIXATION’ should be available for end users’ personalization (the field is not visible, but it can be made visible by an end user).

Now we need to create our own Field Catalog in Field Catalogs for Transportation Cockpit app.

Let’s find and run Field Catalogs for Transportation Cockpit app.

Since we are going to change a Freight Order list, let’s open the existing Filed Catalog.

We need to collect a value of ‘Content Object’ field. This field determines the list/hierarchy of Transportation Cockpit app and we need to define it when we create our own Field Catalog.

Now let’s create our own Field Catalog and use the collected value ‘33’ of ‘Content Object. The field ‘Configuration ID’ should be defined automatically.

The next step is to find ‘Driver’ and clear the check box ‘Display (Default)’ to make this field unavailable for end users.

Now we need to find ‘PURCH_ORG_ID’ and set the check box ‘Display (Default)’ to make this field visible for end users.

Let’s change the field ‘Sequence’ for ‘PURCH_ORG_ID’. It should be ‘4’.

As you see the system changed the sequence of the field. Now this field takes the fourth place.

Finally, we need to find ‘FIXATION’ and set the check box ‘Display (Personalisation)’ to make this field available for end users’ personalization.

The last step is to set our Field Catalog to our Page layout. Let’s find and run Define Page Layouts for Transportation Cockpit app.

I will select my own Page Layout. If it is needed, you can create your own one.

Let’s open the area where we are going to assign the Field Catalog.

Now we can set our Field Catalog in the field ‘Field Catalog Name’ and save the result.

Let’s have a look at the Transportation Cockpit. As you see, the field ‘Driver’ is not visible.

The field ‘PURCH_ORG_ID’ is visible.

The field ‘FIXATION’ is not visible, but available for end users.

Scenario #2. How to add a custom field of Freight Order in Transportation Cockpit app.

In this section we will add a custom field of Freight Order in Transportation Cockpit app. This blog does not describe the process of enhancing Freight Order and we will use a custom field of Freight order which has been already added to Business Object TOR. If you need a guide how to add a custom field to SAP TM Business Object, leave it in the comments 😊

Now let’s have a look at a Freight order Extension Include structure in BOBF transaction, we are going to make the custom field ‘ZENH_ENTRY_DATE’ visible.

Now we need to create our own Field Catalog in Field Catalogs for Transportation Cockpit app (this step is the same as described in Scenario #1).

The next step is to find the custom field ‘ZENH_ENTRY_DATE’ and set the check box ‘Display (Default)’ to make this field visible for end users.

Finally let’s change the field ‘Label’ to a name which we want to see for the custom field ‘ZENH_ENTRY_DATE’ in Transpiration Cockpit app.

The last step is to set our Field Catalog to our Page layout (this step is the same as described in Scenario #1).

Now we can look at the Transportation Cockpit. As you see, ‘ZENH_ENTRY_DATE’ is visible.

Scenario #3. How to add a native custom field in Transportation Cockpit app.

In this section we will add a native custom field in Transportation Cockpit app. The native custom field means it is not part of a Freight Order structure, but it is used to show data in Transportation Cockpit from related internal tables/Business Objects, or it is used to work with Freight Order fields via Transportation Cockpit in more convenient way (e.g. working with Timestamps).

First, we need to enhance a max structure of our Field Catalog. Let’s open the existing Filed Catalog and define the max structure which is related to our Content Object.

Now we can open SE11 transaction and change the max structure.

The next step is to choose the group where we want to add our native custom field.

Now we can choose Append Structure and define the append name and confirm.

Also, we need to enter a short description.

The next step is to maintain the enhancement category.

Now we can add our native custom field ‘ZZ_UI_MAX_DATE’ with the following parameters (of course, you need to define your own name and a type of the custom field).

The last step is to activate the max structure.

There are no errors and the status is ‘Active’, it means we are ready to create our own Field Catalog with the native custom field (this step is the same as described in Scenario #1).

In our own Field Catalog we can find the native custom field ‘ZZ_UI_MAX_DATE’ and set the check box ‘Display (Default)’ to make this field visible for end users.

Also let’s change the label of the native custom field ‘ZZ_UI_MAX_DATE’.

The last step is to set our Field Catalog to our Page layout (this step is the same as described in Scenario #1).

Now we can look at the Transportation Cockpit. As you see, ‘ZZ_UI_MAX_DATE’ is visible.

If you need to implement a custom logic to get data for the native custom field in Transpiration Cockpit app, you can enhance a standard method ADAPT_DATA of FBI view exit class. 

If you need to implement a custom logic to save data into Business Object from the native custom field in Transpiration Cockpit app, you can enhance a standard method ADAPT_CHANGE_LOG of FBI view exit class.

If you need a guide how to implement a custom logic of FBI views in Transpiration Cockpit app, leave it in the comments 😊

I hope this SAP Blog would be helpful for you to implement Field Catalogs for Transportation Cockpit  app easily and without any complications. For more information, please visit the following links:

Enhancements for Manual Planning and Transportation Cockpit | SAP Help Portal

Field Configuration for the Transportation Cockpit | SAP Help Portal

Learn more about SAP Premium Engagement and how a global team of experts can help you to get the most out of your SAP solutions.

If you already are a Premium Engagement customer, contact your Technical Quality Manager (TQM) to discuss how Premium Engagement services can help you with a custom-tailored and efficient service portfolio to make your process mining initiative a success!

#CoE EMEA SCM1

entry.title: Lease Amortization Land and land improvement
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

Hope you are doing well...!

It is my understanding that leased land and land improvements are not required to be reported under IFRS 16. Consequently, there is no depreciation applicable to land lease agreements, which means that such depreciation cannot be applied to land and land improvements for the purpose of capitalizing the Right of Use (ROU) Asset. The business incurs expenses related to the enhancement of land, such as parks, football fields, and other facilities. How should IFRS 16 reporting be approached in this context, considering the absence of depreciation, or alternatively, if depreciation is applicable, how should it be treated as an ROU?

Best Regards,

Sridhar

entry.title: Re: sybase 17 db how to repair error validating table in...
entry.content: As you can see in the Application Event log, the database has a checksum error on a page. This means that the page as written is not equal to the page that has been read based on the checksum.  Do you have a backup and recovery strategy for this database?

entry.title: MFA in IAS Admin Console
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We are looking to enable MFA for IAS Admin Console so that the administrators can login via a secure mechanism & not be entering password manually.

Understand we can configure MFA at the application level ( under Applications & Resources) & choose the Admin console->Risk Based Authentication & then enable Two factor authentication. 

Can you please let us know if this is the standard way to enable MFA at application level or is there any other best practices to be followed .

Please help to share.

Thanks
Regards

Vidya

entry.title: Re: Transform Your GenAI-Based BTP Experience: Digital Assistant with Data Learning & Adaptive P
entry.content:
Hi @LeandroRibeiro,

Thanks for your interest and feedback! In fact, when we started this, the first prototype was done with SAP Build Apps as the UI, just because it is so easy and quick to put something together.

My colleague Jim and I ran a hands-on session using our first learning at last year's TechEd and the material (including the SAP Build Apps project file) is still available:
https://github.com/SAP-samples/btp-extension-s4-material-availability/tree/asugTC23

You can try it out on trial/free tier. We moved on from there, however. While it is an API call to the LLM, we now use the GenAI Hub on BTP (wasn't available when we started) and also have additional security and privacy built-in with the CAP LLM plug-in by my colleague Rui's team:
https://github.com/SAP-samples/btp-ai-core-simplifying-timesheet-tracking-task-in-s-4hana-cloud?files=1 

While we built an own UI and a CAP application for this, the next step would be leveraging SAP Joule as the UI by extending it with SAP Build Joule Studio which will be released in Q1 2025:
https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-builders-discussions/when-will-sap-joule-studio-be-available-beta-access-inquiry/td-p/13949186 

Best regards,
FRANK

entry.title: SAP Commerce & Consumer Industries 2024 Year in Review
entry.content:
Reflecting on 2024: Another Landmark Year for SAP Commerce Cloud and Consumer Industries!

SAP has had a long and successful history of enabling businesses in consumer industries to navigate complexities and drive innovation.  2024 is no different and in many ways a milestone year!  SAP Commerce Cloud and many products that support Consumer Industries has been on fire, rolling out hundreds of cutting-edge capabilities, all designed to elevate customer experiences, streamline operations and delivering industry specific innovations for our customers.  

Curious? Keep reading to explore all the innovations and milestones we achieved!

SAP Commerce Cloud named Leader in Gartner Magic Quadrant for Digital Commerce for 10th year in a row

For the tenth consecutive year, SAP has proudly been named a Leader in the 2024 Gartner Magic Quadrant for Digital Commerce.  But there is more! SAP has also been recognized as a leader by IDC for both B2B and B2C Digital Commerce and honored with the TrustRadius Buyer’s Choice Award. While it’s always wonderful to receive such accolades, our true motivation lies in empowering our customers to drive profitable growth. Be sure to check out our customer stories, organized by industry, to see how businesses have realized financial and operational benefits with SAP Commerce Cloud.

SAP Commerce Cloud - Cyber Week Results

We had another spectacular Cyber Week to kick off the biggest shopping season. SAP Commerce Cloud:

  • Powered $12.2B in Gross Merchandise Value (GMV), a +49.5% year-over-year (YoY) increase
  • Drove +18% increase in average order value and processed +23.4% YoY increased order volume.
  • Delivered 59.3% YoY growth in GMV on Black Friday, 71.9% YoY growth in GMV over Cyber Weekend and 49.5% YoY growth in sales per minute We achieved these impressive results with 100% uptime!

Expanding Retail and Fashion Possibilities with S/4HANA

Building on our momentum, the SAP S/4HANA 2408 Cloud Release for Retail and Fashion showcases SAP’s dedication to delivering innovative, value-driven solutions. Focused on simplifying operations and addressing industry-specific challenges, this release empowers businesses to streamline their processes and scale globally.

With features like enhanced returns management and cross-docking optimization, retailers can reduce complexity and accelerate delivery times. Tailored innovations for the fashion industry, such as season determination enhancements, ensure that businesses stay aligned with customer demands. Additionally, the expansion into Switzerland, Portugal, Canada, and Spain extends our best practices to a broader global audience, supporting their journey toward operational excellence.

By integrating innovation, industry expertise, and global reach, SAP continues to redefine what’s possible in retail.

Retail Predictive Planning for a Rapidly Changing World

Fulfilling orders efficiently depends on having the right products available at the right time, and that’s where our Predictive Planning and Inventory Orchestration solutions shine. These tools integrate forecasting and replenishment logic directly into operations, ensuring that if demand shifts, inventory adapts. By tying into the SAP Customer Activity Repository, predictive planning solutions help unify commerce data and demand signals across channels.  Additionally, improved events management streamlines exceptions handling and planning schedules. By integrating predictive planning into daily operations, businesses can respond to demand shifts proactively, ensuring stock availability and reducing the risk of overstock or stockouts. We’re especially grateful to our co-innovation partners who have collaborated with us to achieve shared success and bring these capabilities to life.

Omnichannel Retail - A Foundation of Data and Insights through SAP Customer Activity Repository

SAP Customer Activity Repository, a powerful data, predictive planning, and omnichannel platform, ensures all operational data—from sales transactions and promotions to inventory levels and forecasts—received its regular update. We’ve focused on enhancing performance, adding a new promotion pricing service, and improving analytics across the board. Improvements in unified demand forecasting, and multichannel analytics contribute to a more holistic view of customer behavior and operational performance.

Consumer Products:  Drive Profitable growth through comprehensive Revenue Management

Great operations need to connect seamlessly with financial strategy, and that’s where SAP Revenue Growth Management (RGM)for Consumer-Packaged Goods (CPG) businesses comes into play. CPGs can now:

  • Easily define targets and KPIs for all your retail partners, ensuring a clear path to achieving sales objectives.
  • Streamlined Collaboration helps to break down organizational silos with seamless, cross-functional collaboration that keeps every stakeholder aligned.
  • API-First Integration: Integrate both in-house and partner solutions effortlessly, thanks to an API-first approach that simplifies connectivity and interoperability.
  • Efficient Spend-Activity Management: Quickly create, copy, manage, and approve spend activities while generating corresponding pricing conditions, ensuring a fast and accurate promotion cycle.
  • Unified Sales Planning: Plan both promoted and non-promoted sales within a single application, making it easier to maintain a holistic view of all your commercial activities.
  • Financial Execution with S/4HANA Cloud: Manage on-invoice and off-invoice liabilities directly through S/4HANA Cloud to maintain full financial control and clarity throughout the trade promotion process.

Built on SAP BTP, RGM leverages modern, cloud-native architecture to deliver scalability and flexibility, helping businesses adapt to fast-changing environments.

Because these features blend into existing finance and supply chain processes, decision-makers enjoy a clear, end-to-end picture of how their trade promotions influence the bottom line. Rapid spend-activity management, API-first integrations, and unified sales planning mean there’s no more guessing—just smart, data-driven action.

With RGM, businesses maximize promotion effectiveness, ensuring every dollar spent translates into measurable sales and margin growth.

From Financial Settlement to the Final Mile
After capturing new sales and securing growth, companies must accurately settle contracts, commissions, and payouts. Settlement Management/Condition Contract Management advancements let organizations set up internal commission agreements, track settled amounts and send documents automatically — streamlining the close-out process so teams can focus on strategy instead of paperwork.

Meanwhile, on the operational side, enhanced Last Mile Distribution (LMD) ensures that once goods are picked and packed, they reach customers smoothly. Updates this year brought better batch handling, refined authorization controls, and support for more traditional paper processes when needed. In a world where the last step often defines the customer’s lasting impression, LMD helps deliver on promises made at the start of the order cycle.

Going Global: Trade Management with Intelligence and Insight
For businesses operating across borders, complexity is the norm. Global Trade Management (GTM) integrates logistics, compliance, and financial requirements into a single streamlined process. This year, we focused heavily on analytics and building toward SAP Business AI capabilities. Consistent with the customer centricity of the other product areas, GTM teams continuously listened to customer feedback to inform priorities and future roadmap items. The result will be a more informed, more agile global trade operation—one that’s ready for the challenges of international commerce.

SAP Commerce Cloud: Continuous Innovation and New Capabilities

Throughout 2024, we continued to provide our customers with differentiating and innovative capabilities, enhancing our unified commerce solution. This year, through our Continuous Innovation release updates, we have introduced over 150 new capabilities. These enhancements span the core platform, Composable Storefront, Order Management Services, integration areas, AI tools, partner solutions, and more. We also finalized two new SaaS packaged business capabilities that will launch soon -- SAP Open Payment Framework and Search Service.

Let's get into some of the highlights!

Commerce Cloud Core Platform

We have added major enhancements to the core platform to improve the security and overall reliability.

  • Scalable sandboxes and consumption credits allows you to selectively ramp up or dial down specific services within non-production environments. SAP Commerce Cloud, consumption credits are the virtual currency (Capacity Units) that you use to self-scale with your non-production environments.

  • Canary deployment takes blue-green deployments a step further by routing a percentage of traffic to the new code version in a parallel production environment for up to 12 hours. This provides added confidence by giving you time to validate and ramp up gradually before going fully live. Canary deployment uses SAP Commerce Cloud, consumption credits.
  • Zone redundancy ensures that a disruption in one zone will not jeopardize your production environment. Already available to all at no cost, benefits include added resilience for your data centers, greater reliability for clusters, and increased availability for critical workloads such as search and storefront operations. Zone redundancy has been implemented for Azure SQL databases, the SAP Commerce Cloud application, along with key infrastructure, such as compute and load balancers.
  • Premium Disaster Recovery minimizes the data loss and amount of time to be back to regular online operations if a major disruption were to occur. It is now available for self-service purchase in SAP Commerce Cloud through your Consumption Credits.

Commerce Cloud Composable Storefront

We introduced new capabilities to our decoupled headless storefront that exclusively leverages SAP Commerce Cloud REST APIs. The updates include:

  • Assisted Service enhancements that provide customer service agents with a quick and efficient customer 360-degree view to provide exceptional service and problem resolution to their customers, and a quicker way to access customer order details.
  • B2B improvements that allow your customers to more easily track and edit quotes as well as configure complex Built-To-Order products with improved user experience that shows updated pricing and options in real-time.
  • Site security during customer registration with preconfigured support for Google reCAPTCHA v2. This added layer of security prevents brute-force attacks in registration and prevents bots from spamming websites.

Order Management Services
SAP Order Management Services unifies buying and fulfillment channels. The composable suite of cloud-native services enable distributed order management. We added many new features, including the following:

  • Workflow to automate the handling of exceptions. If an issue arises during the fulfillment request, the system can re-trigger the fulfillment in some problematic status and apply actions such as re-sourcing or sending a notification to the employee.
  • Live insights for on time performance that aggregates all the order data in a single place.

  • Workflow loops simplify orchestration by applying sophisticated workflows to an even broader set of order data with significantly fewer steps.
  • Branching nodes with a multitude of conditions allows for more control when orchestrating orders with deeper decision capabilities. This provides greater visibility into order fulfillment, allows responses to late customer change requests, and reduces returns by preventing unnecessary shipments.

  • SAP Order Management Sourcing and Availability added support for sets and bundles, the ability to review past sourcing information to improve operations in a unified commerce environment, and contextual updates to provide alternate sourcing results.

Comprehensive and Integrated SAP Suite

New integrations across the SAP portfolio optimize commerce operations, improve business workflows, and make sure you are creating seamless and exceptional customer experiences. Integration with SAP S/4HANA provides an undisputed advantage that unifies front and back-end processes to connect demand to supply and deliver engaging experiences across the customer lifecycle.

  • SAP S/4HANA Sales integration now provides flexible order adjustment capabilities and customers get automatically notified of order changes that are rejected by your ERP. These provide a single source of truth for orders, streamline order management and customer notification, and simplifies B2B order planning.
  • SAP Service Cloud integration improves customer retention and satisfaction by enabling SAP Composable Storefront users to request help and engage with customer service representatives by capturing and managing Service Tickets online.

  • SAP CPQ integration allows B2B storefront buyers to access documents with detailed specifications, price information, and terms and conditions leading to a better and more informed B2B purchasing experience.
  • Customer Data integration allows for a 360-degree view of the customer. This provides you with the customer insights needed to deliver highly personalized content and promotions.
  • Synchronous integration between SAP Commerce Cloud and SAP Order Management Services allows for faster and more efficient order processing, real-time order visibility and reduced overall operational costs.

For an in-depth look at the business advantages of using SAP Customer Experience solutions with SAP ERP, read the paper, Analyzing the Economic Benefits of Using AI-Powered SAP CX Solutions Natively Connected to SAP ERP.

Differentiated AI Capabilities through CX AI Toolkit

Artificial Intelligence/Generative AI was the hot topic this year and we delivered many new features that enhance the customer experience during product discovery and save your business users time through automated processes and workflows.

  • “Ask the product” feature allows your customers to ask specific product-related questions directly on the storefront and get the answers they are looking for through AI-generated replies based on product data managed in the backend. This could be anything from the fabric or cleaning instructions for clothing, to the color and brightness specifications of a light bulb, to the fit and sizing information of a shoe. Any information you have about a product can be made available.

  • Visual Search now goes beyond the retail industry cover all industries. Visual Search improves product discovery by delivering quick and accurate search results based on AI image detection. Let’s say you have an image of a nice dress that you are searching for. Simply upload it and let AI give you recommendations of either that exact dress or similar dresses that you might like. Product discovery has never been easier.
  • Product Image Designer lets you automatically create captivating new product images from simple text descriptions. The Background Editor allows you to transform or remove existing product image backgrounds, staying on-brand and boosting engagement. Imagine launching a new home décor product. How efficient would it be to have a background of a nice living room generated automatically from a text prompt, just as it would look like with a professional photo shoot? This is exactly the type of use case the Image Designer enables.

  • SAP Intelligent Product Recommendation is a cloud-native Business AI tool specially designed for manufacturers and distributors dealing with complex products. SAP Intelligent Product Recommendation uses historical data, paired with machine learning, to suggest the most suitable standard or customizable products, increasing sales and conversions.

Open Payment Framework (coming in Q1 2025)

SAP Commerce Cloud, open payment framework, provides a central payment "hub", where key Payment Service Providers, including Stripe, Adyen, and Worldpay have already been pre-integrated, so you do not need to worry about manual integrations anymore.

Organizations can easily adopt this plug-and-play solution, reducing integration complexity. The framework is extensible and headless, enabling independent operation of front and back ends, catering to diverse channel needs for global businesses, and adding innovative solutions seamlessly.

SAP Commerce Cloud, open payment framework, covers common payment requirements and end-to-end payment processes across authorization, capture, refunds, and re-authorization while automatically staying current with growing security and compliance standards. It also ensures customers have the freedom to pick their preferred payment method, which enhances the checkout experience.

Search Service (coming in Q1 2025)

At SAP, our goal is to provide our customers with all the flexible search and merchandising tools they need to enable intelligent product discovery, drive sales and increase conversions. And by modernizing our architecture ourselves, we are adhering to the same recommendations that we provide to our customers, that the move to independent cloud services managed by SAP is the best way forward to achieve a lower TCO, less development efforts and easier maintenance.

A key component of this strategy is our soon to be launched Search Service. It provides comprehensive search and merchandising features, including flexible search indexing & scoring, facets/synonyms/stemming/spelling checking capabilities with word suggestions, product variant grouping, and visual search bar configuration. It is provided as a SaaS service with 99.95% availability, a flexible API, automatic upgrades and out-of-the-box integration with SAP Commerce Cloud. It is ready for global expansion with multi-currency & multi-language support.​

And in combination with Intelligent Selling Services, we enable Intelligent Search, delivering highly relevant results to your customers with recent and trending search suggestions and using AI to boost the product results by individual interest and intent.​

SAP Recommerce
SAP Recommerce drives revenue and sustainability by capturing the second-hand online market for your products. We added many new enhancements this year to help manage the trade-ins.

  • Configurable product attributes: Admin users can create up to 15 customer-specific product attributes for trade-in items, so the application closely matches your business needs and displays the correct product details and attributes to customers on the storefront.
  • Item IDs: Workbench users can assign a distinct barcode or unique ID to a trade-in item. Users can view a page listing all trade-in items and filter by item’s barcode or unique ID value.
  • Sustainability data: We now provide clear visibility of sustainability data and impact for business users and customers with admins being able to import and view sustainability data for each SKU and search for products or metrics in the data.
  • Configurable workflows: We improved the usability and management of trade-in products through intuitive business tools and workflows. Admins can create custom workflows to suit specific programs, such as “clean only”, “clean and repair”, “repair only”. When the workbench user selects a condition indicator, the application generates the associated workflow steps, from “check-in” to “ready to restock”.

Telco and Utilities Accelerator
We bring more enhancements to this industry-specific capability to help organizations in the telco and utilities industries grow their revenue and transform faster.

  • Subscription services information. Customers can get a comprehensive understanding of their subscription services, for both fixed and personalized selections. Customers can also easily track modifications to their service contract selections, including pricing updates for greater transparency.
  • Support for utilities retail journeys from simple products to complex bundles.
  • New design of digital B2C energy sales in Telco and Utilities Accelerator Composable Storefront.
  • Improved integration with SAP S/4HANA for sales orders (utilities only)

Partner Solutions
Our growing partner ecosystem on the SAP Store adds to the already robust offering SAP Commerce Cloud through solution extensions.

  • Oneview Unified Commerce Platform extends SAP Order Management Services into the physical store to maximize profitability and improve customer outcomes. Oneview Unified Commerce Platform provides shopping convenience by supporting all in-store fulfillment types for web-based orders, and associate-optimized workflows allow for efficient picking and packing of orders and preparing them for customer pickup or direct shipment to the customer.
  • FrontNow is a virtual AI advisor that boosts customer engagement by providing your shoppers with personalized interactions. The AI advisor represents your brand in engaging, one-on-one conversations, and supports 85 languages for tailor-made shopping and service experiences.
  • ChannelEngine harnesses AI to optimize management across 950+ online sales channels and international marketplaces. By combining the strengths of SAP Order Management Services and ChannelEngine, you can operate commerce unbounded. You get centralized shopper order processing and access to rich insights into consumer demand, sales performance, and market trends.

This is just a snapshot of the many features we have launched this year. For comprehensive details, please refer to our documentation. 

Upcoming Events
Stay connected and learn more about the latest innovations by joining our upcoming webcasts:

  • SAP Commerce Cloud Q4 2024 Tech-Focused Update: January 14, 2025
  • SAP Commerce Cloud Q4 2024 Release Recap: January 22, 2025.

As we close this remarkable year, we wish you happy holidays and look forward to delivering even more exciting features in 2025!

Please share your feedback in a comment and follow SAP Commerce Cloud on SAP Community to connect and engage in the new year with fellow industry peers and e-commerce experts!

Looking Ahead: Confidence in 2025 and Beyond
The innovations delivered in 2024, from smarter order management to the latest S/4HANA release, demonstrate SAP’s unwavering commitment to helping businesses thrive in an ever-changing world. As we head into 2025, our focus remains on delivering features that drive value, ensure scalability, and create seamless customer experiences.

Together, we look forward to shaping the future of commerce and consumer industries!

Happy holidays and here’s to a fantastic year ahead!

entry.title: Re: Capacity Utilization for a group
entry.content:
One option is to look into Handling Resources and add MD in RESOURCELOCATIONPRODUCT to get some usage in CAPAUSAGE.
Another is to add a normal Production Resource and add additional rows on PRODUCTIONRESOURCE for each source id to get usage in PCAPAUSAGE.
So when a product is planned, it's consuming capacity for 2 resourses at the same time.

entry.title: Re: Can old BW 731 system consume data from EC Payroll in...
entry.content: Hi, Roland, thank you very much for your help! It was a group of EC Payroll system consultants from SAP company working on a project for our company to set up a new ECP system that told us that SAP will not support RSA extractor to our old BW system and suggested we look for other options. The note 2232584 you mentioned is very helpful. But we still cannot replicate the ODP dataources. In EC Payroll system, we ran ABAP BS_ANLY_DS_RELEASE_ODP so the requested datasources 0HR_PY_PP_1/2 and some custom created generic datasources are available for ODP framework with entries in table ROOSATTR and “X” for Expose_External. We gave our Basis team to set up EC Payroll system as an ODP source system for our BW system. However, in Source System, we only see a single “ODP” folder instead of multiple folder (i.e. ODP-BW, ODP-SAP (extractors), ODP-SLT Queue…). When we go into this new ODP source system of EC Payroll, the application component folder tree is totally different from what we see in EC Payroll. It seems to be from somewhere local within the BW system. The Replicate datasource option is not available. Hence, we try to create a new datasorce manually. It forces us to select an Operational Data Provider from an empty list. The option of entering fields for datasource is greyed out. So, we cannot manually create a datasource. It seems to me that it is expecting a BW type of ODP datasource even though the source system is EC Payroll. Our BW system production system currently has a BPC system set up as BW type of ODP source system, which is working fine. Other things I tried is to run Run program SAP_RSADMIN_MAINTAIN per note 2577071 to avoid issues caused by authorization. What is the reason for us not be able to create/replicate datasource under ODP source system from EC Payroll source system? Is only BW type ODP source system available for our old BW system (BI_BASIS 7.31 with SP 30 and SAP_BW 7.31 with SP 30)? Or the ODP source system set up was not done correctly or something was missing? Thanks! Jenny

entry.title: SAPUI5 Custom App: Shipment Tracking with SAPUI5 and Google Maps Integration
entry.content:
Business Case:

In the logistics industry, real-time shipment tracking is a necessity. Traditional systems often lack a visual component, leaving businesses without critical insights into routes, distances, and potential delays. Shipment Tracking Application, built with SAPUI5 and Google Maps, addresses these challenges by combining route visualization, detailed instructions, and multilingual support into a single application.

Features:

1. Interactive Map Integration

  • Seamless integration with Google Maps to display real-time shipment routes.
  • Dynamic updates based on user input, such as shipment ID.

2. Step-by-Step Directions

  • Instructions and distances for each step in the route.
  • Multilingual support to cater to global users.

3. Shipment Search

  • Simple search functionality to retrieve shipment routes using shipment IDs.

4. Real-Time Distance Calculation

  • Display total distance for the shipment route instantly.

Setup Instructions

To integrate Google Maps, you need to set up an API key:

  1. Go to the Google Developers Console.
  2. Create or select a project:
    • If you don’t have an existing project, create a new one.
  3. Enable Google Maps JavaScript API:
    • Search for "Google Maps JavaScript API" in the library and enable it.
  4. Create API Credentials:
    • Navigate to the "Credentials" page.
    • Generate a Browser Key for your application.
  5. Set API Restrictions:
    • Restrict the key to your application’s domain for security purposes.

Example API URL for integration:

 

https://maps.googleapis.com/maps/api/js?key=YOUR_API_KEY&callback=initMap

 

Replace YOUR_API_KEY with the key generated in the Google Developers Console. 

XML View

 

<mvc:View controllerName="shiptrack.controller.View1"
    xmlns:mvc="sap.ui.core.mvc" displayBlock="true 
    xmlns:core="sap.ui.core"
    xmlns="sap.m" xmlns:l="sap.ui.layout">
    <Page id="page" title="Shipment Tracker">
        <VBox>
            <SearchField id="searchField" placeholder="Enter Shipment ID" search=".onFindShipment" />
            <Text id="totalDistanceText" text="Total Distance: {route>/distance}" />
            <l:Splitter orientation="Horizontal" width="100%" height="500px">
                <l:Splitter>
                    <!-- Directions List -->
                    <List id="directionsList" items="{route>/steps}">
                        <CustomListItem>
                            <VBox>
                                <Text text="{route>instruction}" />
                                <Text text="{route>distance}" />
                            </VBox>
                        </CustomListItem>
                    </List>
                </l:Splitter>
                <l:Splitter>
                    <!-- Map Container -->
                     <Core:HTML id="mapContainer" width="70%" height="500px" />
                </l:Splitter>
            </l:Splitter>
        </VBox>
    </Page>
</mvc:View>

 

Full Controller Code:

 

sap.ui.define([
    "sap/ui/core/mvc/Controller",
    "sap/m/MessageToast",
    "sap/ui/model/json/JSONModel"
], function (Controller, MessageToast, JSONModel) {
    "use strict";

    return Controller.extend("shiptrack.controller.View1", {
        onInit: function () {
            this._map = null;
            this._directionsService = null;
            this._directionsRenderer = null;

            const oModel = new JSONModel({ steps: [], distance: "" });
            this.getView().setModel(oModel, "route");

            this._loadGoogleMaps().then(() => {
                this._initializeMap();
            }).catch((error) => {
                console.error("Google Maps could not be loaded:", error);
                MessageToast.show("Unable to load Google Maps. Please try again later.");
            });
        },

        _loadGoogleMaps: function () {
            return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
                if (typeof google !== "undefined" && google.maps) {
                    resolve();
                    return;
                }
                const script = document.createElement("script");
                script.src="https://maps.googleapis.com/maps/api/js?key=YOUR_API_KEY&libraries=geometry";
                script.async = true;
                script.defer = true;
                script.onload = resolve;
                script.onerror = reject;
                document.head.appendChild(script);
            });
        },

        _initializeMap: function () {
            const mapContainer = this.byId("mapContainer").getDomRef();
            this._map = new google.maps.Map(mapContainer, {
                center: { lat: 0, lng: 0 },
                zoom: 2
            });
            this._directionsService = new google.maps.DirectionsService();
            this._directionsRenderer = new google.maps.DirectionsRenderer({ map: this._map });
        },

        onFindShipment: function () {
            const shipmentId = this.byId("searchField").getValue();
            if (!shipmentId) {
                MessageToast.show("Please enter a shipment ID.");
                return;
            }

            const startCoords = { lat: 30.0444, lng: 31.2357 };
            const endCoords = { lat: 31.2023, lng: 29.9014 };

            this._displayRoute(startCoords, endCoords);
        },

        _displayRoute: function (startCoords, endCoords) {
            const request = {
                origin: startCoords,
                destination: endCoords,
                travelMode: google.maps.TravelMode.DRIVING
            };

            const oModel = this.getView().getModel("route");

            this._directionsService.route(request, (result, status) => {
                if (status === google.maps.DirectionsStatus.OK) {
                    this._directionsRenderer.setDirections(result);

                    const route = result.routes[0];
                    const distance = route.legs[0].distance.text;
                    const steps = route.legs[0].steps.map((step) => ({
                        instruction: step.instructions.replace(/<[^>]+>/g, ''),
                        distance: step.distance.text
                    }));

                    oModel.setProperty("/steps", steps);
                    oModel.setProperty("/distance", distance);

                    MessageToast.show(`Total distance: ${distance}`);
                } else {
                    MessageToast.show("Unable to retrieve directions.");
                }
            });
        }
    });
});

 

Final Demo Video

Watch the application in action: 


Conclusion

With the combination of SAPUI5 and Google Maps API, our Shipment Tracking Application delivers a powerful solution for real-time logistics management. By following the provided setup instructions and integrating the Google Maps API securely, you can create an efficient and user-friendly tool for your business. 

Feel free to implement this application in your SAP projects and customize it as per your needs.
Happy coding! 😉

entry.title: Living SAP Joint Venture Accounting (JVA) - Part 2
entry.content:
After demonstrating how to record the agreement between the operator and non-operator parties, we will now illustrate how to document the ventures to which the Joint Operation Agreement ratios and other JOA information will be applied.

By doing so, we will address our fourth question:

4. How to record or extend agreement activities?

Continuing with our imagination, as friends who decided to start a business together and have already agreed on the terms and share percentages in the Joint Operating Agreement (JOA).

We are now ready to launch either one venture ( Project ) or more ventures (Projects) that are controlled under our outlined agreement . Each venture will consist of phases, known as Equity Types and under each equity type, we will link the operator and the share percentages for each partner, based on what we agreed upon in our JOA.

In such a case the venture is agreement under the joint operating agreement in which two or more parties agree to work together for a common purpose.

Remembering the initial concepts of JVA where : 

 1. We are going to be partners in case that operational activities are capital intensive, involve high risk, and possibly long payback periods.

2. This enables us to spread the risk by sharing in operational costs and revenues in proportion to our investments.

In order to conduct the risk sharing of operation costs we are going to  determine it by the date when this costs accrued and this can be known based costs document date in SAP. 

Then we should determine each document is related to each venture and related to which document date to determine exactly this document happens under each phase ( Equity type ) and each ( Equity group).

Equity types can be actual or logical activity which is linking venture and JOA equity groups in Oil and Gas industry we commonly classified Equity groups as: 

  • Research and Development ( R&D)

Exploration 

Development

  • Engineering and design

Pre-FEED (Preliminary Front-End Engineering Design)

Front End Engineering Design (FEED)

  • Construction, and production

Production Oil

Production Gas

  • Decommissioning

However, Any project or project phase that would be funded and executed only by our company this should be considered a corporate effort and neither the costs or revenues related to this phase are going to be shared.

Let's have a look on sample Equity group that should be maintained on venture master data under transaction code GJVV:

As highlighted, equity groups will link financial transactions to the allocation percentages of partner shares based on the transaction date. However, to connect financial transactions, such as expenses or revenues, to a specific venture, we need to extract the venture data from the cost objects assigned to each transaction.

SAP cost objects like:

  • Cost centers

  • Internal orders

  • Projects and work breakdown structures (WBS elements)

  • Profit centers

Can have the venture data either required or optional based on JVA cost object setup.

SAP JVA is integrated across all cost objects in the system. This is important for linking financial documents with the relevant venture information, whether in the Financial module or other modules like HCM, MM, Project Systems, etc.

Once a posting done, JVA captures ALL financial and cost postings in real time from standard modules and converts them to JV format.

However, at this stage, the financial document may contain a lot of JVA data, but it doesn’t provide the information needed to determine whether the document should be split between partners or if it is not part of this sharing agreement. 

In general, documents that are part of the sharing agreement have a billing nature, meaning the cost is to be divided between partners, and at the same time, each partner's portion must be formally communicated to them.

The costs to be divided between partners ( shared costs ) are called recoverable or Cutback related costs as after the operator pay for it, he should recover, cutback or claim those costs back from non-operator partners.

The costs that should be communicated formally ( legally Invoiced ) to partners are part of billing process and called related to billing.

The term billable expenses in business generally represent the recoverable and relating to billing expense and vice versa for non-billable. 

The billing indicator in JVA is assigned to each posting line in the financial documents. Based on this, the system determines whether the transaction should be recovered and whether it should be included in the billing process.

The financial documents line items can inherit the recovery indicator at the following level of postings:

1. Document type 

2. GL Account master data

3. Cost object 

4. Manual override during posting ( In the financial document )

5. JVA integration manager

Each venture can have a different set of posting rules that control the GL accounts used for transactions related to this venture, such as cash requests from partners (cash calls) or cost recovery processes (cutback of expenses). These posting rules are part of the venture master data.

The funding process for a venture can be managed by a funding group, which determines the bank data (accounts, house bank, and bank ID) to be used for this venture or a group of ventures."

at this stage we can now determine for each financial document either it should be recovered, be part of billing process , related to which venture ( based on the cost object ) and what is the recovery ratios ( based on the equity type and date stored in the venture master data).

Summary:

We’ve already covered how to record the agreement between operator and non-operator parties in a Joint Operation Agreement (JOA). Now, we focus on documenting the ventures to which JOA ratios and other relevant details will apply.

For each venture, we define phases (Equity Types) where partners' share percentages are set according to the JOA. These ventures, controlled under the JOA, help manage joint operational risks, and cost-sharing is based on the transaction date in SAP.

In SAP, financial transactions are linked to ventures through cost objects (like cost centers, internal orders, and projects). These cost objects capture the venture data needed for proper cost allocation.

The system determines if costs should be recovered and / or billed based on recovery indicators, and based on the posting rules set in the venture master data it determine how the related JVA document to be post.

Finally, the funding process for ventures is managed by a funding group, which controls the bank details for each venture.

In the next blog, we should continue answering common partnership questions that JVA can cover.

entry.title: SAP GUI for Java’s 25th Anniversary Edition brings back multi-colored icons
entry.content:
In December 1999, the first public beta version of SAP GUI for Java has been published, followed by the general availability in June 2000 announced at TechEd in Las Vegas.
At that time, the platforms were Mac OS 8, OS/2, HP-UX, Solaris, Linux, AIX and Windows 95/98/NT/00) and the theme was EnjoySAP.

SAP GUI for Java 4.6D with EnjoySAP theme

25 years later, SAP GUI for Java 8.10 rev 1 is available and can be considered an anniversary edition. It brings back the multi-colored icons that have been missed by many since Belize. 

Icons in Signature Design, Corbu, Blue Crystal, Belize, Quartz, Horizon and "Horizon+"

When using Horizon themes they can be activated in the Preferences dialog on the Themes panel with the checkbox "Use multi-colored icons".

Activate multi-colored icons in preferencesABAP Workbench with multi-colored icons in Evening HorizonA list of more new features and bug fixes in SAP GUI for Java 8.10 rev 1 is available in SAP note 3485691.

entry.title: The settlement rule receiver : Issue with S/4 HANA EAM WOs
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

Requirement: The settlement rule receiver should be selected based on the Account Assignment maintained in the Technical Object. For example, if a Cost Center (CTR) is maintained, then upon releasing the Work Order (WO), the system should designate the Cost Center as the receiver. Similarly, if a Work Breakdown Structure (WBS) is maintained, then upon releasing the WO, the system should designate the WBS as the receiver.

Changes Which I have performed in SPRO as In Settlement profile

  1. Valid receivers: CC: Settlement Optional and WBS Element: Settlement Optional
  2. Default Object Type in Default Values: Kept as Blank ( I tried this with CTR as well but no luck)

While trying to release WO one with CTR maintained in account Assignment of Technical Object and one with WBS maintained in account Assignment of Technical Object, Order is rejected to release since Settlement rule is not maintained.

Expectation is:

  • If the order type is linked to a Cost Center, the system will prefer the Cost Center as the receiver.
  • If the order type is linked to a WBS Element, the system will prefer the WBS Element as the receiver.

Am I missing anything here?

BR,

Riyaj

Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to define housekeeping settings or purchase addit...
entry.content:
Hello, 

1) To identify the Cloud ALM application that is using the most DB HANA size, please refer to the following KBA: ##3432729 - How to Monitor SAP Cloud ALM Memory Usage

2) After that, you can consult the following guide, which will help you adjust the housekeeping for each application: https://support.sap.com/en/alm/sap-cloud-alm/operations/expert-portal/housekeeping.html

3) If you require more memory, you have the option to subscribe to SAP Cloud ALM, tenant extension. This service provides an additional SAP Cloud ALM tenant with 8 GB of SAP HANA memory and 8 GB of monthly outbound API data transfer. For more information, please refer to the guide on Getting Additional SAP Cloud ALM Tenants.

Best regards

entry.title: formula for splitting fullname as firstname and lastname in BO 4.3
entry.content:
i was trying to split  a fullname with the below formula
as firstname , lastname (wherein fullname including with the middle name in it.)

Below Formula worked fine: 

First Name=Left(Substr([Name];Length(Substr([Name];1;Pos([Name];" "))+2);Length([Name]));Pos(Substr([Name];Length(Substr([Name];1;Pos([Name];" "))+2);Length([Name]));" "))

Last Name=Right(Substr([Name];Length(Substr([Name];1;Pos([Name];" "))+2);Length([Name]));Pos(Substr([Name];Length(Substr([Name];1;Pos([Name];" "))+2);Length([Name]));" ")+2)

QUESTION : Is there anyother simpler way we can achieve this and also Mid funtion is not available in Webi?

LAST_NAME: Mid(PATRN_NAME,1,InStr(PATRN_NAME,',')-1)

entry.title: Distribution rule data cannot reflect to G/L account report
entry.content:
In SAP Business ByDesign, after setting and running the overhead distribution rule for August and September in a testing environment, the results did not reflect in the G/L Item report. Guidance is needed on whether the "Total Costs by Cost Center and Assigned Projects" report can be used to verify the distribution.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Distribution rule data cannot reflect to G/L account...
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

In SAP Business ByDesign, if you want to verify the results of your overhead distribution, you can use several reports to check if the overheads have been distributed and posted correctly. Here are a couple of reports you might find useful:

  • Total Costs by Cost Center and Assigned Projects:
    Yes, this report is a good starting point. It provides a summary of total costs incurred by cost centers and projects, including allocations. Check if the overhead amounts have been distributed correctly to the assigned cost centers or projects.

  • Cost Center - Line Items:
    This report provides detailed transactional data for each cost center. Verify if the overhead amounts are posted to the respective cost centers in this report.

  • Allocation Run Log:
    Navigate to the Overhead Run log. Check if the system successfully processed the allocation run and if there are any errors or unprocessed items.

  • Project Cost and Revenue Overview:
    If overhead is distributed to projects, this report can help confirm the amounts allocated to specific projects.

When reviewing these reports, make sure you: - Check the correct posting periods (for August and September in your case). - Verify the overhead accounts and cost elements. - Look for any error messages or logs from the overhead allocation run for additional clues. If you continue to have issues, it might be helpful to revisit the configuration of your overhead distribution rules to ensure everything is set up correctly. This might involve checking the allocation bases, percentages, and validity periods of the rules.

Also, posting of the Overhead distribution rule will have the journal entry type : 00073 - Distribution. So, can you try using the report G/L line item and filter it for the journal entry type.

entry.title: Manufacturing Type ( Discrete and Process )
entry.content:
Hello,I know that production manufacturing methods include discrete manufacturing and process manufacturing and so on.

Then what's the difference between discrete manufacturing and process manufacturing ? More precisely, why would companies that seem better suited to process manufacturing adopt a discrete manufacturing?

To tell you in advance, I know the concept of most production methods. because I've seen a lot of related tables.

So, Please stop saying that the finished product can be broken down into raw materials or uses process orders or production orders as work instructions, and that you use Recipe and Formulas instead of BOM. Please don't say anything that seems to be on the website table.

My question is why would jam, laundry detergent, and bread-making companies choose Production Order instead of Process Manufacturing. Does bread and jam break down into flour again? From what I've seen on the internet, No. It can't be done.
But why are they making bread with the Production Order? Let me know if you know why they made that choice.

In my prediction, it's the CEO's mind, so we can't know everything.
But even though Process Manufacturing is a more suitable product(jam or bread), I wonder what difference it makes to choose Discrete Manufacturing.

Thank you for taking the time to think about it.

entry.title: Request License SAP Software Appliance (S/4Hana Onpremise)
entry.content:
Hi SAP Support,
Can we request permanent license for SAP Software Appliance S/4Hana (On-Premise?

Thanks
Trilo


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Request License SAP Software Appliance (S/4Hana Onpre...
entry.content:
Dear customer,

To generate license keys for SAP S/4HANA on-premise edition, follow the how-to steps below:

  1. Go to SAP for Me > License Keys
  2. Select/search for the installation number to use 
  3. Click + Add System
  4. Product: SAP S/4HANA 
  5. Version: Select from drop-down list
    • SAP S/4HANA 2023
    • SAP S/4HANA 2022
    • SAP S/4HANA 2021
    • SAP S/4HANA 2020
    • SAP S/4HANA 1909
    • SAP S/4HANA 1809
    • SAP S/4HANA 1709
    • SAP S/4HANA 1610
    • SAP S/4HANA On-Premise Edition 1511
  6. System ID: Enter any 3-character system ID
  7. System Name: Optional
  8. System Type: Select from drop-down list
  9. Database: Select from drop-down list
  10. Operating System: Select from drop-down list
  11. Click Continue
  12. Click + Add License Key
  13. License Type: Select from drop-down list
  14. Hardware Key: Enter hardware key found in SLICENSE
  15. Valid until: 31-12-9999
  16. Click Add
  17. Select license key from the list
  18. Click Generate (at the bottom)
  19. Install S/4HANA license key in SLICENSE

To obtain further information on how to request a license key for SAP S/4HANA, kindly refer to KBA 2366280 - How to request a license key for SAP S/4HANA (all versions) - SAP for Me

entry.title: Re: How to get Values in Fiori Template Material BOM
entry.content:
1. You can maintain the different Production version (C223) to choose different  BOM according to the lot size.

For Example:

Production version: 0001    - >  BOM Alternative BOM: 01    Lot size: 0-100

Production version: 0002    - >  BOM Alternative BOM: 02    Lot size: 101-200

Production version: 0003    - >  BOM Alternative BOM: 03    Lot size: 201-99999999

2. If you want to mass change BOM, there is no standard app to upload a file to change the BOM, you should contact your developer to create a new program.

But you can use "Bill of Material Mass Maintenance" to mass change the BOM.

Links: SAP Fiori Apps Reference Library
